xanesnet.models.e3ee_full package¶

E3EEFull: multi-absorber E3-equivariant model.

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.E3EEFull(model_type, out_size, max_z, atom_emb_dim, atom_hidden_dim, atom_layers, local_cutoff, rbf_dim, energy_rbf_dim, scatter_dim, latent_dim, head_hidden_dim, e3nn_irreps, e3nn_irreps_message, e3nn_lmax, out_mlp_layers, use_invariant_branch, use_attention_branch, use_equivariant_branch, use_eq_attention_branch, use_conv_branch, use_eq_conv_branch, use_path_branch, use_absorber_mask, residual_scale_init, attention_heads, attention_rbf_dim, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, att_cutoff, conv_use_gate=True, fusion_mode='cat')[source]¶

Bases: Model

Multi-absorber E3-equivariant model.

Predicts a XANES spectrum for every atom in the batch simultaneously, suitable for training across multiple absorber sites per structure. The equivariant atom encoder is absorber-agnostic; absorber selectivity can optionally be applied via use_absorber_mask.

Seven optional prediction branches are supported (at least one must be enabled): invariant energy branch, invariant attention, equivariant attention, invariant convolution, equivariant convolution, late equivariant head, and 3-body path aggregator. Active branch outputs are fused according to fusion_mode: "cat" concatenates them along the last dimension before the final MLP head, while "gated" combines them with an energy-conditioned GatedBranchFusion before the final MLP head.

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • out_size (int) – Number of energy-grid points (nE).

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by element embeddings.

  • atom_emb_dim (int) – Dimension of the element embedding.

  • atom_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the equivariant encoder MLPs.

  • atom_layers (int) – Number of equivariant interaction blocks in the encoder.

  • local_cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff for the encoder graph in A.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for local-graph distances.

  • energy_rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for energy encoding.

  • scatter_dim (int) – Intermediate scatter feature dimension (path branch).

  • latent_dim (int) – Output dimension of each prediction branch.

  • head_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of branch-specific MLPs, gated fusion MLPs, and final head.

  • e3nn_irreps (str) – Irreps of encoder node features.

  • e3nn_irreps_message (str) – Irreps of encoder intermediate messages.

  • e3nn_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order in the encoder.

  • out_mlp_layers (int) – Number of layers in the final head MLP.

  • use_invariant_branch (bool) – Enable the invariant energy branch.

  • use_attention_branch (bool) – Enable the invariant attention branch.

  • use_equivariant_branch (bool) – Enable the late equivariant head branch.

  • use_eq_attention_branch (bool) – Enable the equivariant attention branch.

  • use_conv_branch (bool) – Enable the invariant convolution branch.

  • use_eq_conv_branch (bool) – Enable the equivariant convolution branch.

  • use_path_branch (bool) – Enable the 3-body path aggregator branch.

  • fusion_mode (str) – Branch fusion strategy. "cat" preserves the existing concatenate-and-MLP head, while "gated" uses learned energy-conditioned soft gates before the final head.

  • use_absorber_mask (bool) – If True, restrict query/receiver/center-site selection in the attention, convolution, and path branches to absorber atoms, and zero final predictions on non-absorber atoms via absorber_mask.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial scale of the encoder residual connections.

  • attention_heads (int) – Number of attention heads.

  • attention_rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for attention distances.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for attention branches.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Irreps of equivariant values in attention/convolution branches.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff for the attention/convolution graph in A.

  • conv_use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style scalar gate in convolution branches.

__init__(model_type, out_size, max_z, atom_emb_dim, atom_hidden_dim, atom_layers, local_cutoff, rbf_dim, energy_rbf_dim, scatter_dim, latent_dim, head_hidden_dim, e3nn_irreps, e3nn_irreps_message, e3nn_lmax, out_mlp_layers, use_invariant_branch, use_attention_branch, use_equivariant_branch, use_eq_attention_branch, use_conv_branch, use_eq_conv_branch, use_path_branch, use_absorber_mask, residual_scale_init, attention_heads, attention_rbf_dim, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, att_cutoff, conv_use_gate=True, fusion_mode='cat')[source]¶

Initialize E3EEFull.

forward(x, mask, absorber_mask, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_weight, edge_vec, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec, energies, path_center, path_j, path_k, path_r0j, path_r0k, path_rjk, path_cosangle)[source]¶

Forward pass producing a spectrum for every atom.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (int64, padded), shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor) – Per-atom absorber indicator, shape (B, N).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge lengths in A, shape (E,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Edge displacement vectors in A, shape (E, 3).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Attention-graph source indices, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Attention-graph destination indices, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Attention-graph distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Attention-graph displacement vectors in A, shape (E_att, 3).

  • energies (Tensor) – Energy grid, shape (n_abs, nE) (only nE is used).

  • path_center (Tensor) – Flat site index per path into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_j (Tensor) – Flat j atom index into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_k (Tensor) – Flat k atom index into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_r0j (Tensor) – Absorber-to-j distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_r0k (Tensor) – Absorber-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_rjk (Tensor) – j-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_cosangle (Tensor) – Cosine of the j-absorber-k angle, shape (P,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Predicted XANES intensities for every atom, shape (B, N, nE) (padded). The caller is expected to mask out atoms without ground truth.

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize all Linear and Embedding weights.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Name of the weight initializer registered in WeightInitRegistry.

  • bias_init (str) – Name of the bias initializer registered in BiasInitRegistry.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the weight initializer factory.

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return model signature as a Config.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.e3ee_full module¶

Multi-absorber E3-equivariant model (E3EEFull).

class xanesnet.models.e3ee_full.e3ee_full.E3EEFull(model_type, out_size, max_z, atom_emb_dim, atom_hidden_dim, atom_layers, local_cutoff, rbf_dim, energy_rbf_dim, scatter_dim, latent_dim, head_hidden_dim, e3nn_irreps, e3nn_irreps_message, e3nn_lmax, out_mlp_layers, use_invariant_branch, use_attention_branch, use_equivariant_branch, use_eq_attention_branch, use_conv_branch, use_eq_conv_branch, use_path_branch, use_absorber_mask, residual_scale_init, attention_heads, attention_rbf_dim, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, att_cutoff, conv_use_gate=True, fusion_mode='cat')[source]¶

Bases: Model

Multi-absorber E3-equivariant model.

Predicts a XANES spectrum for every atom in the batch simultaneously, suitable for training across multiple absorber sites per structure. The equivariant atom encoder is absorber-agnostic; absorber selectivity can optionally be applied via use_absorber_mask.

Seven optional prediction branches are supported (at least one must be enabled): invariant energy branch, invariant attention, equivariant attention, invariant convolution, equivariant convolution, late equivariant head, and 3-body path aggregator. Active branch outputs are fused according to fusion_mode: "cat" concatenates them along the last dimension before the final MLP head, while "gated" combines them with an energy-conditioned GatedBranchFusion before the final MLP head.

Parameters:

  • model_type (str) – Model type string (passed to base class).

  • out_size (int) – Number of energy-grid points (nE).

  • max_z (int) – Maximum atomic number supported by element embeddings.

  • atom_emb_dim (int) – Dimension of the element embedding.

  • atom_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of the equivariant encoder MLPs.

  • atom_layers (int) – Number of equivariant interaction blocks in the encoder.

  • local_cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff for the encoder graph in A.

  • rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for local-graph distances.

  • energy_rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for energy encoding.

  • scatter_dim (int) – Intermediate scatter feature dimension (path branch).

  • latent_dim (int) – Output dimension of each prediction branch.

  • head_hidden_dim (int) – Hidden dimension of branch-specific MLPs, gated fusion MLPs, and final head.

  • e3nn_irreps (str) – Irreps of encoder node features.

  • e3nn_irreps_message (str) – Irreps of encoder intermediate messages.

  • e3nn_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order in the encoder.

  • out_mlp_layers (int) – Number of layers in the final head MLP.

  • use_invariant_branch (bool) – Enable the invariant energy branch.

  • use_attention_branch (bool) – Enable the invariant attention branch.

  • use_equivariant_branch (bool) – Enable the late equivariant head branch.

  • use_eq_attention_branch (bool) – Enable the equivariant attention branch.

  • use_conv_branch (bool) – Enable the invariant convolution branch.

  • use_eq_conv_branch (bool) – Enable the equivariant convolution branch.

  • use_path_branch (bool) – Enable the 3-body path aggregator branch.

  • fusion_mode (str) – Branch fusion strategy. "cat" preserves the existing concatenate-and-MLP head, while "gated" uses learned energy-conditioned soft gates before the final head.

  • use_absorber_mask (bool) – If True, restrict query/receiver/center-site selection in the attention, convolution, and path branches to absorber atoms, and zero final predictions on non-absorber atoms via absorber_mask.

  • residual_scale_init (float) – Initial scale of the encoder residual connections.

  • attention_heads (int) – Number of attention heads.

  • attention_rbf_dim (int) – Number of Gaussian RBF bases for attention distances.

  • attention_lmax (int) – Maximum spherical-harmonics order for attention branches.

  • attention_irreps (str) – Irreps of equivariant values in attention/convolution branches.

  • att_cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff for the attention/convolution graph in A.

  • conv_use_gate (bool) – If True, apply a PaiNN-style scalar gate in convolution branches.

__init__(model_type, out_size, max_z, atom_emb_dim, atom_hidden_dim, atom_layers, local_cutoff, rbf_dim, energy_rbf_dim, scatter_dim, latent_dim, head_hidden_dim, e3nn_irreps, e3nn_irreps_message, e3nn_lmax, out_mlp_layers, use_invariant_branch, use_attention_branch, use_equivariant_branch, use_eq_attention_branch, use_conv_branch, use_eq_conv_branch, use_path_branch, use_absorber_mask, residual_scale_init, attention_heads, attention_rbf_dim, attention_lmax, attention_irreps, att_cutoff, conv_use_gate=True, fusion_mode='cat')[source]¶

Initialize E3EEFull.

forward(x, mask, absorber_mask, edge_src, edge_dst, edge_weight, edge_vec, att_src, att_dst, att_dist, att_vec, energies, path_center, path_j, path_k, path_r0j, path_r0k, path_rjk, path_cosangle)[source]¶

Forward pass producing a spectrum for every atom.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Atomic numbers (int64, padded), shape (B, N).

  • mask (Tensor) – Valid-atom mask, shape (B, N).

  • absorber_mask (Tensor) – Per-atom absorber indicator, shape (B, N).

  • edge_src (Tensor) – Flat source indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_dst (Tensor) – Flat destination indices into B*N, shape (E,).

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – Edge lengths in A, shape (E,).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – Edge displacement vectors in A, shape (E, 3).

  • att_src (Tensor) – Attention-graph source indices, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dst (Tensor) – Attention-graph destination indices, shape (E_att,).

  • att_dist (Tensor) – Attention-graph distances in A, shape (E_att,).

  • att_vec (Tensor) – Attention-graph displacement vectors in A, shape (E_att, 3).

  • energies (Tensor) – Energy grid, shape (n_abs, nE) (only nE is used).

  • path_center (Tensor) – Flat site index per path into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_j (Tensor) – Flat j atom index into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_k (Tensor) – Flat k atom index into B*N, shape (P,).

  • path_r0j (Tensor) – Absorber-to-j distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_r0k (Tensor) – Absorber-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_rjk (Tensor) – j-to-k distance in A, shape (P,).

  • path_cosangle (Tensor) – Cosine of the j-absorber-k angle, shape (P,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Predicted XANES intensities for every atom, shape (B, N, nE) (padded). The caller is expected to mask out atoms without ground truth.

init_weights(weights_init, bias_init, **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize all Linear and Embedding weights.

Parameters:

  • weights_init (str) – Name of the weight initializer registered in WeightInitRegistry.

  • bias_init (str) – Name of the bias initializer registered in BiasInitRegistry.

  • **kwargs – Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the weight initializer factory.

Return type:

None

property signature: Config¶

Return model signature as a Config.
